explanation: This command is a direct replication of the true positive test command and is not a bypass. It is provided for testing purposes.
The '-Command' flag is substituted with the shorter '-c' equivalent, which is functionally identical in PowerShell.
The value 'scecli' is modified to 'scecli\0*' to simulate a malicious payload while still matching the Sigma rule's detection criteria for testing.
This is the original true positive test command and is not a bypass. It is included for completeness in testing.
Quotes around the registry path are omitted, which is allowed in many Windows command-line contexts and still valid for testing.
Quotes around the value name are omitted, which is allowed in some Windows command-line contexts and still valid for testing.
